---
title: AI-Driven Analytics for Energy-Efficient and Cost-Aware Data Center Planning
format:
  poster-typst: 
    size: "36x24"
    poster-authors: "Pouya Zolfi"
    departments: "Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering"
    institution-logo: "./images/MU.jpg"
    footer-text: "Marquette University"
    footer-url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/pouyazolfi"
    footer-emails: "pouya.zolfi@marquette.edu"
    footer-color: "ebcfb2"
bibliography: ref.bib
csl: apa.csl
citeproc: true
---

```{=typst}
#set text(font: "Helvetica")
```

# Background

- Data centers use substantial energy for computing and cooling.

- Operators must balance workload demand, electricity cost, and carbon impact.

- This study combines descriptive analytics and AI to support efficient and cost-aware data center planning.


# Dataset Overview

- The dataset includes hourly workload, electricity price, server parameters, and ADS cooling-system states.

- These data support integrated analysis of:
  - power demand
  - operating cost
  - carbon emissions
  - cooling performance


# Methodology

We use a three-step framework.

## Step 1: Operational Analytics

Let

$W_t = W_t^(int) + W_t^(batch)$

denote hourly total workload.

We analyze:

- workload demand
- electricity price
- server requirement
- IT and cooling power
- total facility power
- hourly electricity cost
- hourly carbon emissions


## Step 2: Cooling-System Modeling

The adsorption cooling system (ADS) is modeled using:

- inputs:
  - generator temperature $T^(gen)$
  - condenser temperature $T^(cond)$

- outputs:
  - coefficient of performance $COP$
  - cooling capacity $q^(cap)$


## Step 3: AI Surrogate Model

We train a Random Forest multi-output model:

$$
(widehat(COP), widehat(q^(cap))) = f(T^(gen), T^(cond))
$$

to approximate the ADS operating map.


# Key Equations

$$
Electricity Cost = Energy Consumption times Electricity Price
$$

$$
$$

$$
Carbon Emissions = Energy Consumption times Carbon Intensity
$$

$$
$$

$$
R^2 = 1 - frac(sum_i (y_i - hat(y)_i)^2, sum_i (y_i - bar(y))^2)
$$


# Workload and Electricity Price

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_1_Hourly_Workload.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 1.* Daily workload pattern.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_2_Electricity_Price.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 2.* Hourly electricity price.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Key Insight:* Workload and price peaks do not fully align, creating opportunities for cost-aware scheduling of flexible demand.
]
```

# Power, Cost, and Carbon Results

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_3_Power_Breakdown.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 3.* Facility power breakdown.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_4_Cost_and_Carbon.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 4.* Cost and carbon emissions.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Observation:* Higher workload increases both IT and cooling demand, which raises cost and carbon emissions.
]
```

# Cooling-System Performance Maps

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_5_ADS_COP_Heatmap.svg", width: 6.4in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 5.* ADS COP map.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_6_ADS_Capacity_Heatmap.svg", width: 6.4in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 6.* ADS capacity map.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Key Finding:* Maximum efficiency and maximum capacity occur in different operating regions.
]
```


# AI Model Validation

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_7_AI_Validation_COP.svg", width: 5.9in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 7.* AI validation for COP.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_8_AI_Validation_Capacity.svg", width: 5.9in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 8.* AI validation for capacity.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Model Result:* The Random Forest surrogate predicts both COP and cooling capacity accurately and can support optimization and control.
]
```


# Conclusion

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  • Workload and price profiles reveal scheduling opportunities  
  • Facility power, cost, and emissions vary substantially over time  
  • ADS performance is strongly nonlinear  
  • A Random Forest surrogate accurately predicts COP and cooling capacity
]
```
